commit.message:
Fix bot 409 conflict: retry with backoff instead of crashing

When Telegram returns 409 (another getUpdates request), wait
5-30s and retry instead of crashing the process, which caused
Docker to instantly restart and repeat the conflict loop.

commit.message:
feat: Lightning zap — send sats to a DID (#155)

* feat: Add Lightning zap — send sats to a DID via service endpoint

Adds publish-lightning, unpublish-lightning, and lightning-zap commands.
Recipients publish a Lightning service endpoint in their DID document;
senders resolve the DID, request an invoice from the endpoint, and pay it.

Closes #154

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: Add unit and client tests for Lightning zap methods

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: Add missing client tests for poll voters and options branches

Adds tests for addPollVoter, removePollVoter, listPollVoters, and
options variants for getVault, listVaultItems, getVaultItem,
getDmailMessage, and listDmailAttachments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Add Tor hidden service for Drawbridge and route zaps over Tor

Drawbridge now auto-discovers its .onion address via a shared Docker
volume and returns it when publishing Lightning service endpoints.
Zap requests route through Tor SOCKS proxy for .onion endpoints.
Renamed lightningZap to zapLightning and added alias support.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Address PR review feedback

- Add optional memo to zapLightning across all layers
- Safe req.body?.id on publish/unpublish API routes
- Thread payer identity (id) through zap API route and client
- Validate amount as positive integer on Drawbridge zap endpoint
- Add SSRF protection: enforce https for non-.onion, block private IPs
- Use dynamic port in entrypoint Tor hostname fallback

commit.message:
fix: decrypt context mismatch with AWS Encryption SDK and improve error messages (#21)

## Summary
- **Fix encryption context mismatch bug**: The AWS Encryption SDK adds
`aws-crypto-public-key` to ciphertext headers when using key commitment
algorithms (`REQUIRE_ENCRYPT_REQUIRE_DECRYPT`). DynamoDB only stores the
4 application-level keys. Replace strict `!=` comparison with a subset
check that verifies all expected keys are present while ignoring
legitimate SDK-added keys. Fixes both `decrypt` and `rotate-key`
commands.
- **Add top-level error handler to decrypt command**: AWS/crypto errors
now produce human-readable Rich-formatted messages instead of raw Python
tracebacks. Catches `EncryptionContextMismatchError`,
`ChecksumMismatchError`, `ConfigurationError`,
`ClientError`/`BotoCoreError`, and generic `EnvaultError`.
- **Improve exception messages**: `EncryptionContextMismatchError` now
shows which keys differ. `ChecksumMismatchError` includes actionable
guidance.

## Test Plan
- [x] `pytest tests/unit/ -v` — 90 tests pass
- [x] `ruff check src/ tests/` — lint clean
- [x] `ruff format --check src/ tests/` — format clean
- [x] `mypy src/envault/` — type check clean
- [ ] Manual: `envault decrypt test_file.txt` against real AWS should
succeed (no more context mismatch)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add VirusTotal scanning for agent versions (#77)

## Summary
- Trigger VirusTotal scanning on agent publish (both `publish` and
`publishInternal` mutations), with the same text-file skip optimization
used for skills
- Add agent-specific scan actions (`submitAgentScan`,
`pollAgentResult`), mutations (`setAgentScanStatus`, `flagAgent`), and
queries (`getAgentVersionFiles`)
- Update the moderator scan queue to show both skill and agent pending
scans, with kind badge and correct detail page links
- Add `retriggerAgentScan` mutation for moderators to retry failed agent
scans

## Test plan
- [ ] Publish an agent with only text files (e.g., AGENT.md) → verify
scan status is "skipped"
- [ ] Publish an agent with binary files → verify scan is submitted to
VirusTotal
- [ ] Check scan queue page shows agent items with "agent" badge and
correct link
- [ ] Retry a failed agent scan from the scan queue

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Rewire backend to use Scope REST API with RAG pattern

  (context/search, context/summary, ask, searchAcrossProjects)

  inject tagged results into system prompt, then call GitHub Models API
- Update /api/projects to fetch real entity/endpoint counts from Scope

Flow: user question → parallel Scope search across repos → merge & tag
results → inject into Copilot system prompt → return answer + sources

(enabled, context, loadOnFirstForeground) into a single configuration
object. Deprecates the flat featureFlagsEnabled/featureFlagsContext params
on MixpanelOptions in favor of the new setFlagOptions() builder method.

flags are automatically fetched on the first app foreground event.

commit.message:
v1.22.50: Revert ClearData PAGE_READWRITE — causes vtable corruption hang

PAGE_READWRITE during ClearData lets the engine corrupt the sentinel
vtable (offset +0x00). ClearData then calls virtual functions on
sentinel forms → corrupted vtable → infinite loop (105% CPU, 0 mem
growth, 20+ min hang confirmed).

Keep PAGE_READWRITE only for initial load (first FORM-ZEROPAGE redirect)
where it works safely (no virtual calls on sentinels during loading).
ClearData keeps PAGE_READONLY with write-skip flood — but manual "New
Game" from UI doesn't trigger ClearData at all (only resetGame=true
does), so this path should rarely be hit in practice.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: decode URI component in fetchImageBuffer to handle spaces in avatar path

The faceless avatar directory name "faceless avatar" has a space that gets
URL-encoded to %20 when parsed through new URL().pathname, causing file
not found errors in Docker. decodeURIComponent resolves this.

Normalize DDD context labels across all 95 Erlang source files (BT-1094) (#1134)

## Summary

Normalizes 22 ad hoc DDD context labels to the 7 canonical contexts
defined in `docs/beamtalk-ddd-model.md`:

- **Actor System Context** (2 files)
- **Concurrency Context** (1 file)
- **Object System Context** (69 files)
- **Hot Reload Context** (2 files)
- **Workspace Context** (11 files)
- **REPL Session Context** (28 files)
- **Compilation (Anti-Corruption Layer)** (7 files)

### Key changes
- Standardized comment format to `%%% **DDD Context:** ...` (triple-%)
everywhere
- Added valid DDD context label reference table to
`docs/development/erlang-guidelines.md`
- Header-only edits — no functional code changes
- All tests pass, erlfmt clean

### Linear issue

https://linear.app/beamtalk/issue/BT-1094/normalize-ddd-context-labels-across-all-95-erlang-source-files

Introduces isolated git worktree environments per issue in the ADW pipeline.
Each issue now gets a dedicated worktree with deterministic ports (postgres,
backend, frontend) via new actors: CreateWorktree, ConfigureWorktree,
SetupWorktree, StartWorktreeEnv. Tracker persists worktree metadata.
Cron cleans up stale worktrees for closed issues automatically.
